events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: thermal growth offset ignored on hot service, in-service. This is a shaft alignment fault. Thermal growth offset ignored means the alignment was done cold without accounting for thermal expansion, so when equipment heats up, misalignment occurs. This is non-compliant with API 686 which requires thermal growth compensation. In service, this would cause vibration, coupling wear, bearing damage. Component condition: degraded (likely) or failed. Risk: high. Decision: fail.

],
"actions": [
"Shut down or reduce load if vibration/temperature readings indicate distress",
"Perform hot alignment check and measure actual thermal growth",
"Recalculate and re-shim/move equipment to incorporate thermal growth offset per API 686 and laser system manual procedures",
"Re-verify alignment hot and cold, document results for third-party inspection"
],
"rationale": "API 686 requires thermal growth compensation for hot-service machinery alignment. Ignoring the thermal growth offset means the cold alignment is non-compliant and the equipment runs misaligned at temperature, degrading couplings and bearings. In-service evidence of ignored thermal offset is a fail.",
